reasoning: The current marathon world record is 2:00:35, set by Kelvin Kiptum in 2023. Tragically, Kiptum passed away in early 2024, removing the sport's only realistic contender for a sub-two-hour marathon in the near future. The fastest marathon time in 2024 was 2:02:06 (by Sabastian Sawe), which is more than two minutes off the sub-2 barrier. Furthermore, the first major marathon of 2025 (Tokyo on March 2) just posted a winning time no faster than 2:02:23. A drop of more than two minutes from the current active field's best times within the next 10 months is historically unprecedented and highly unlikely without radical (and legal) technological changes. The Manifold market probability of 0.47 appears to be an anomaly, possibly reflecting very low liquidity or anchoring to predictions made prior to Kiptum's death. I estimate the probability of a World Athletics-ratified sub-2-hour marathon by the end of 2025 to be at most 3%.
